section_en: Green Dye, Direct Green Tint, Intense Green Tint for Liqueurs, and Violet

ingredients.name_it: tintura di zafferano o di calendola
ingredients.note: added gradually until desired green color obtained

steps_en: Dissolve indigo carmine in distilled hot water.
Gradually add saffron or calendula tincture until a beautiful green coloration is obtained.

section_en: Green Dye, Direct Green Tint, Intense Green Tint for Liqueurs, and Violet

ingredients.name_en: plant leaves (lemon balm, spinach, hyssop, periwinkle, or bramble)
ingredients.name_it: foglie di piante (melissa, spinaci, issopo, pervinca o rovi)

ingredients.note: double the weight of the leaves

steps_en: Finely pound the leaves in a mortar.
Place the mass in a wide-mouthed bottle and add a double weight of 90° alcohol.
Macerate for 15 days, then press or filter to obtain the tincture.
Optionally stabilize with a weak sodium carbonate solution, then saturate with hydrochloric acid.
notes_en: Color tends to alter, turning yellow with turbidity and sediment unless stabilized.

section_en: Green Dye, Direct Green Tint, Intense Green Tint for Liqueurs, and Violet

ingredients.name_en: spinach or fresh herbs, finely chopped
ingredients.name_it: spinacci o erbe fresche, finamente tagliuzzati

ingredients.note: poured over the plant residue after pressing

steps_en: Macerate spinach or fresh herbs, finely chopped, with distilled water and sodium carbonate for 21 hours.
Press and discard the resulting liquid.
Pour 70% alcohol over the plant residue.
After 12 hours (no longer), press and filter to collect the tincture.
notes_en: Solution is a beautiful green color and harmless; store in opaque glass vessels.

section_en: Green Dye, Direct Green Tint, Intense Green Tint for Liqueurs, and Violet

ingredients.note: added to the pulp then pressed

steps_en: Crush fresh bilberry berries to reduce them to pulp.
